rows.text: The judgment criteria for daily inspection items should be made known to vehicle users.
The "●" and "◆" marks indicate the inspection period required by law, and the "○" and "◇" marks indicate that Honda recommends it.

The "◆" mark indicates "distance items" that can be omitted depending on the mileage since the previous inspection. For 12-month and 24-month inspection items of distance items, such inspection and maintenance may be omitted if the mileage is less than 5,000 km per year, but may not be omitted twice in a row. For "business use" and "private-use cargo," etc., if the annual mileage is less than 8,000 km (2,000 km in 3 months and 4,000 km in 6 months when the inspection period is 3 months and 6 months, respectively), the relevant inspection and maintenance may be omitted, but not twice in a row.
● Rough roads (bumpy roads, gravel roads, unpaved roads)
Rough road surface where the driver feels impact (rush sensation) on his/her body
Road surfaces that have many opportunities to be hit by stones, ruts, etc.
● Many driving on mountain roads and uphill descent roads.
(Approximate number of times the brakes are used due to frequent climbing and descending)
Repeatedly run at outside temperatures below freezing.
When there is a lot of idling
If any of the conditions from A to E applies to more than approximately 30% of the total distance traveled, the vehicle is classified as Severe Condition.

Function of exhaust gas reduction device during deceleration Function of the deceleration fuel cutoff device

Warm up the engine and check that the idling RPM is constant. Raise the engine speed to approximately 3,000 rpm and confirm that the injectors make an operating sound (ticking sound). When the accelerator pedal is released, confirm that the injector operation noise stops momentarily.
Function of exhaust gas recirculation system Inspect the EGR valve.

Idle the engine and disconnect the coupler from the EGR valve. Confirm that the engine becomes sluggish (engine speed fluctuates) when the positive (+) terminal of the battery is connected to the No. 5 terminal of the connector on the EGR valve side and the negative (-) terminal to the No. 1 terminal. If the engine does not malfunction, remove the EGR valve and inspect its function by itself.
